{{Short description="Branch of classical mechanics describing motion without reference to forces}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Kinematics
| image = Projectile motion.svg
| caption = [[Projectile motion]] is a classic kinematic problem: the trajectory is a [[parabola]] under uniform [[gravity]].
| topics = [[Classical mechanics]], [[Motion]], [[Velocity]], [[Acceleration]], [[Dynamics (mechanics)]]
}}

'''Kinematics''' is the branch of [[classical mechanics]] that describes the [[motion]] of points, bodies, and systems of bodies without considering the [[force]]s that cause them to move. It is often referred to as the "geometry of motion." The study of forces and their relation to motion is [[dynamics (mechanics)]].

== Fundamental quantities ==
Kinematics uses four primary quantities:

* '''Position''' <math>\mathbf{r}(t)</math> — location in space as a function of time
* '''Displacement''' <math>\Delta\mathbf{r} = \mathbf{r}_f - \mathbf{r}_i</math>
* '''Velocity''' <math>\mathbf{v}(t) = \frac{d\mathbf{r}}{dt}</math> — rate of change of position
* '''Acceleration''' <math>\mathbf{a}(t) = \frac{d\mathbf{v}}{dt} = \frac{d^2\mathbf{r}}{dt^2}</math> — rate of change of velocity

== Equations of motion (constant acceleration) ==
For constant acceleration <math>a</math> in one dimension:
:<math>v = v_0 + at</math>
:<math>x = x_0 + v_0 t + \frac{1}{2}at^2</math>
:<math>v^2 = v_0^2 + 2a(x - x_0)</math>
:<math>x = x_0 + \frac{1}{2}(v_0 + v)t</math>

== Types of motion ==

=== Rectilinear motion ===
Motion along a straight line.

=== Projectile motion ===
Motion under uniform [[gravity]] with initial velocity at an angle <math>\theta</math>:
* Horizontal: <math>x(t) = v_0\cos\theta \, t</math>
* Vertical: <math>y(t) = v_0\sin\theta \, t - \frac{1}{2}gt^2</math>
* Range: <math>R = \frac{v_0^2\sin 2\theta}{g}</math>
* Maximum height: <math>h = \frac{v_0^2\sin^2\theta}{2g}</math>

=== Circular motion ===
* Angular displacement: <math>\theta</math>
* Angular velocity: <math>\omega = \frac{d\theta}{dt}</math>
* Angular acceleration: <math>\alpha = \frac{d\omega}{dt}</math>
* Relation to linear quantities: <math>v = \omega r</math>, <math>a_t = \alpha r</math>, <math>a_c = \frac{v^2}{r}</math>

=== Relative motion ===
Velocities in different reference frames:
:<math>\mathbf{v}_{AC} = \mathbf{v}_{AB} + \mathbf{v}_{BC}</math>

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Acceleration]]
* [[Classical mechanics]]
* [[Dynamics (mechanics)]]
* [[Motion]]
* [[Velocity]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Kinematics| ]]
[[Category:Classical mechanics]]
[[Category:Motion (physics)]]
